sambodhiparāyanā.meaning: destined for complete enlightenment; having full awakening as their final goal

sambodhiyaṅgesu.meaning: in the limbs of enlightenment; among the factors of awakening
breakdown: Euphonic glide consonant inserted in sandhi.

sambāhanaṁ:.summary: Listed with ucchādana (anointing) and parimaddana (rubbing) among the bodily indulgences a renunciate abstains from.

sameti.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + eti)
breakdown: Goes, comes (√i). Together: comes together, meets.

samma.meaning: friend!, my dear (familiar address); right, properly
samma.note: uncertain: the lesson vocabulary glosses samma as “right” (as in sammā); as an independent word samma is most often a friendly vocative “my dear”.

sammadaññā.meaning: through right knowledge; having rightly understood
breakdown: Final knowledge, full understanding (shortened from aññāya, gerund of ājānāti).

sammadaññāvimutto.meaning: liberated through right knowledge; freed by perfect understanding

breakdown: Liberated, freed (past participle of vimuccati, plural).

sammatta.summary: The eight sammattā are the eight limbs of the Noble Eightfold Path stated as attainments of rightness.
breakdown: Abstract suffix: the state of being right.
sammatta.goenka: The lexicon explains that the eight sammattā — states of righteousness or correctness — are the eight limbs of the Noble Eightfold Path: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right awareness and right concentration. Walking on the path means developing each of these in one's own conduct and practice.
sammatta.note: divergence: DPD lists sammatta also as past participle of sammajjati/sammadati; the lexicon's sense here is the abstract noun from sammā, “rightness”, and that sense is followed.

sammodamānā.meaning: exchanging friendly greetings; on friendly terms, in agreement

breakdown: Potential participle suffix: “fit to be rejoiced in”, pleasant.

sammodiṁsu.meaning: they rejoiced; they exchanged friendly greetings

sammukhāssa.meaning: face to face with him; in his presence
breakdown: Face to face, in the presence of (saṁ + mukha “face”).
Of him/it (genitive singular pronoun, fused in sandhi).

sammukhībhāvā.meaning: from the state of being face to face; owing to the presence of
breakdown: Face to face (sammukhā in compound form).

sammādiṭṭhisuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a discourse)

sammāmanasikāramanvāya.meaning: in consequence of right attention; as a result of rightly fixing the mind
breakdown: Fixing of attention, attending (manasi “in the mind” + kāra “making”).
Undergoing, in consequence of (gerund of anveti).

sammānanāya.gram: feminine abstract noun (instrumental/dative singular), from sammāneti

sammāpaṭipadā.meaning: the right path; right manner of walking, right practice
breakdown: Manner of walking, way of practice, path.
sammāpaṭipadā.goenka: Sammāpaṭipadā is the right manner of walking on the path — not a belief to be accepted but a practice to be undertaken step by step. In this tradition the entire teaching is paṭipadā, something to be walked: practising sīla, samādhi and paññā in one's own life so that the truth is experienced directly.

sammāpaṭipattādhikaraṇahetu.meaning: by reason of correct practice; in consequence of right conduct
breakdown: In consequence of, on account of.
Cause, reason; at the end of a compound: “by reason of”.

sammāsaṅkappagocarā.meaning: dwelling in the sphere of right thoughts; whose pasture is right thought

sammāsaṅkappaṁpahaṁ.gram: phrase: noun (accusative singular) + particle + pronoun (sandhi fusion)
breakdown: Remnant of the particle pi “also”, doubled in sandhi.
Root meaning “to be confused, deluded” (mūḍha > mūḷha).

samodhāna.meaning: combination; collocation; being contained or brought together
breakdown: Root “to place” with action suffix: placing together, combination.

samodhāna to be.note: uncertain: this key is a glossary parsing artifact — the words “to be” from the gloss “to be contained” were attached to the headword samodhāna; see the entry samodhāna.

breakdown: Root “to place” with action suffix: placing together, combination.
Root meaning “to be thrilled, delighted”; causative: to gladden.

sampajañña.meaning: thorough understanding; clear comprehension of impermanence in every sensation
sampajañña.summary: A core meditation term, paired with sati in the formula “ātāpī sampajāno satimā”.
sampajañña.gram: neuter abstract noun (from sampajāna; saṁ + pa + √ñā)
breakdown: Root meaning “to know”. Sampajāna: knowing thoroughly.
Abstract noun suffix: the quality of thorough understanding.
sampajañña.goenka: Sampajañña is thorough understanding — the constant, clear comprehension of impermanence in every sensation. The volumes attest it chiefly through the adjective sampajāno in the classic satipaṭṭhāna formula ātāpī sampajāno satimā, “ardent, with thorough understanding and aware”: the meditator dwells aware with constant thorough understanding of arising and passing. In this tradition sampajañña is not mere mindfulness of activities but the wisdom that arises from observing the impermanent nature of sensations from moment to moment. In the compound sampajānamusāvāda (“deliberate lie”) the same prefix-word sampajāna carries the everyday sense “with full awareness, intentional”.

sampajānamusāvādo.meaning: a deliberate lie; intentional false speech

sampakkhandanalakkhaṇasaddhāpañho, milin.meaning: “The question on faith as marked by leaping forward” — a section of the Milindapañha, in the Khuddakanikāya
breakdown: The question (pañha) on faith (saddhā) whose characteristic mark (lakkhaṇa) is leaping forward (sampakkhandana).
The Milindapañha text (Questions of King Milinda).
In the Khuddakanikāya, the Minor Collection (locative).

sampakkhandanalakkhaṇā.meaning: having the characteristic mark of leaping forward; marked by aspiration

sampannadiṭṭhino.meaning: one endowed with (accomplished in) view; one whose view is perfected
breakdown: Fallen (into), attained (past participle of √pad). Sampanna: endowed with, accomplished.
One who has a view; here: possessing (right) view.
Root “to go, attain” with past participle suffix: attained, endowed.

samparāyahitāya.meaning: for welfare in the future life; for benefit in lives to come
breakdown: The future life, the next world (saṁ + parā + √i “to go on”).
Dative singular ending: “for the sake of”.

samparāyasukhāya.meaning: for happiness in the future life
breakdown: The future life, the next world.
Dative singular ending: “for the sake of”.

samparāyikaṁ.meaning: belonging to the future life; concerning the next world

sampasādanalakkhaṇasaddhāpañho,.meaning: “The question on faith as marked by tranquillization” — a section of the Milindapañha, in the Khuddakanikāya

sampasādanalakkhaṇā.meaning: having the characteristic mark of serenity; marked by tranquillization
breakdown: Serenity, tranquillization (from pasīdati “becomes calm and clear”).
Root “to settle” with action suffix: settling down, becoming serene.

sampattaviratiādīsu.meaning: in (the kinds of abstinence) beginning with abstinence on a given occasion
breakdown: Arrived at, reached (past participle of sampāpuṇāti).
Root “to attain” with abstract suffix: attainment.

sampavati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + pavati)
breakdown: Blows, wafts (√vā). Together: blows forth, spreads fragrance.

sampayogopi.meaning: and association with; even being combined with

samphappalāpo.meaning: frivolous talk; idle chatter, useless prattle
breakdown: Chatter, prattle (pa + √lap “to talk”). (The doubled pp is sandhi doubling.)
samphappalāpo.goenka: Samphappalāpa, frivolous talk, is one of the four unwholesome actions of speech abandoned in sammāvācā, right speech. The volumes describe how one who has given it up speaks at the proper time, speaks what is true and beneficial, and utters words worth treasuring — speech that supports rather than scatters the development of the mind.

breakdown: Chatter, prattle (pa + √lap). (The doubled pp is sandhi doubling.)
